metadata.title: Ribociclib Therapy (Metastatic) - 28 day
metadata.indication: Ribociclib with an aromatase inhibitor or fulvestrant for eligible HR-positive, HER2-negative locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Only entered values are assessed; omitted clinical domains remain explicitly unassessed.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

long_qt_or_significant_cardiac_risk.label: Long-QT syndrome or significant QT/cardiac risk

ast_alt_grade.label: AST/ALT elevation grade (CTCAE)
ast_alt_grade.assessment_guidance: Calculate the multiple of ULN from the measured ALT/AST and the laboratory ULN. Also assess bilirubin and whether cholestasis or another cause is present.

nonhaem_toxicity_grade.label: Other non-haematological toxicity (CTCAE)

options.label: Grade 0 — no toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of other non-haematological toxicity.

options.label: Grade 1 — mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; clinical observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — moderate
options.description: Moderate symptoms or abnormality; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits age-appropriate instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation or prolongation of hospitalisation may be indicated; disabling; limits self-care activities of daily living.

nonhaem_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Use the toxicity-specific CTCAE definition, including intervention required, admission and effect on instrumental/self-care activities of daily living.

renal_band.label: Creatinine-clearance category

options.label: CrCl ≥30 mL/min — no renal dose adjustment
CrCl <30 mL/min — use 33% of original starting dose

renal_band.assessment_guidance: Select the exact NCCP renal tier. Do not infer a finer range than the source protocol.

output_templates.proceed: No encoded contraindication or hold criterion was triggered. Complete normal prescribing and pharmacy checks.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; unentered clinically relevant fields remain unassessed.

action.recommendation: Interrupt until recovery to grade ≤2, then resume one dose level lower.

rules.explanation: Interrupt until recovery to grade ≤2, then resume one dose level lower.

action.recommendation: Interrupt until recovery to grade ≤2, then resume one dose level lower.

rules.explanation: Interrupt until recovery to grade ≤2, then resume one dose level lower.

action.recommendation: Interrupt until ANC recovers to ≥1.0 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the same dose.

rules.explanation: Interrupt until ANC recovers to ≥1.0 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the same dose.

action.recommendation: Interrupt until recovery to grade ≤2, then resume one dose level lower.

rules.explanation: Interrupt until recovery to grade ≤2, then resume one dose level lower.

action.recommendation: Ribociclib is not recommended in haemodialysis.

rules.explanation: Ribociclib is not recommended in haemodialysis.

action.recommendation: Use 33% of the original starting dose for CrCl <30 mL/min.

rules.explanation: Use 33% of the original starting dose for CrCl <30 mL/min.

action.recommendation: Use 67% of the original dose in Child-Pugh B or C.

rules.explanation: Use 67% of the original dose in Child-Pugh B or C.

action.recommendation: Avoid ribociclib until major QT/cardiac risk factors and electrolyte abnormalities are corrected or reviewed.

rules.explanation: Avoid ribociclib until major QT/cardiac risk factors and electrolyte abnormalities are corrected or reviewed.

action.recommendation: Interrupt for QTcF >500 ms; resume at the next lower dose only after recovery, per protocol.

rules.explanation: Interrupt for QTcF >500 ms; resume at the next lower dose only after recovery, per protocol.

action.recommendation: Discontinue for AST/ALT elevation with bilirubin elevation consistent with severe drug-induced liver injury criteria.

rules.explanation: Discontinue for AST/ALT elevation with bilirubin elevation consistent with severe drug-induced liver injury criteria.

action.recommendation: Interrupt for grade ≥3 toxicity until recovery, then resume according to recurrence and dose-modification table.

rules.explanation: Interrupt for grade ≥3 toxicity until recovery, then resume according to recurrence and dose-modification table.

action.recommendation: If a further dose reduction below 200 mg/day is required, discontinue treatment.

rules.explanation: If a further dose reduction below 200 mg/day is required, discontinue treatment.

supportive_care.supportive_medications_label: Moderate/high emetogenic oral therapy antiemetic script — follow NCCP/local policy
premedication_summary: Routine premedication is not usually required; prescribe rescue or prophylactic antiemetics according to individual risk and local policy.
other_supportive_care: Correct potassium, calcium, magnesium and phosphate before treatment.
Repeat ECG at Day 14 of cycle 1 and as clinically indicated.
Avoid grapefruit and review QT-prolonging/CYP3A interacting medicines.

ctcae_standard.reference: CTCAE grading as cited by the NCCP source protocol
ctcae_standard.display_requirement: Every grade-based input includes toxicity-specific criteria and practical assessment guidance. Do not select a grade from the number alone.
